quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 12
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Review — Waves and Optics
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: A ray of light strikes a flat plane mirror such that the angle between the incident ray and the reflective surface is $31^{\circ}$. Calculate the total angular deviation between the path of the incident ray and the path of the reflected ray.
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence is measured from the normal: $90^{\circ} - 31^{\circ} = 59^{\circ}$. By the Law of Reflection, the angle of reflection is also $59^{\circ}$. The total deviation between the two rays is $59^{\circ} + 59^{\circ} = 118^{\circ}$.

questions.question: Two flat mirrors are joined at a right angle ($90^{\circ}$). A light ray enters the system and strikes Mirror 1 at an angle of incidence of $42^{\circ}$. The ray then reflects off Mirror 1 and hits Mirror 2. What is the angle of reflection at the surface of Mirror 2?
options: Exactly $48^{\circ}$ measured relative to the normal of Mirror 2
Exactly $42^{\circ}$ measured relative to the normal of Mirror 2
Exactly $90^{\circ}$ measured relative to the normal of Mirror 2
Exactly $24^{\circ}$ measured relative to the normal of Mirror 2
questions.explanation: The mirrors are perpendicular, so their normals are also perpendicular. The ray reflects off Mirror 1 at $42^{\circ}$, making the complement angle $48^{\circ}$. This becomes the angle of incidence on Mirror 2, so the angle of reflection on Mirror 2 is also $48^{\circ}$.

questions.question: An optical marker is placed vertically in front of a flat plane mirror. If the optical marker is shifted backward by a distance of $1.3\text{ m}$ away from the mirror surface, what is the change in the total distance separating the physical marker from its virtual image?
questions.explanation: In a plane mirror, $d_o = d_i$. Moving the object $1.3\text{ m}$ back increases both the object-mirror and image-mirror distances by $1.3\text{ m}$. Total separation increase $= 1.3 + 1.3 = 2.6\text{ m}$.

questions.question: A light beam propagates from a vacuum into a transparent organic liquid. The speed of light within this liquid is $1.86 \times 10^{8}\text{ m/s}$. Given that the speed of light in a vacuum is $3.00 \times 10^{8}\text{ m/s}$, determine the absolute refractive index of the liquid.

An observer looks vertically downward into a solid synthetic glass slab ($n = 1.54$). A small air bubble trapped near the bottom of the slab appears to be located at a depth of $4.2\text{ cm}$ below the top surface. What is the actual, physical thickness of the glass slab?

A narrow beam of monochromatic light traveling through air ($n_1 = 1.00$) hits the surface of a polished transparent plastic block at an angle of incidence of $46^{\circ}$. The beam refracts as it enters the plastic, moving at an angle of refraction of $27^{\circ}$. What is the refractive index of this plastic block? ($\sin 46^{\circ} = 0.7193$, $\sin 27^{\circ} = 0.4540$)

An industrial fiber optic strand has a core glass index of $1.64$ surrounded by an outer cladding layer. The critical angle for total internal reflection at the core-cladding boundary is $67^{\circ}$. Calculate the refractive index of the cladding material. ($\sin 67^{\circ} = 0.9205$)

A periodic transverse wave propagates along a taut string. An observer tracks a single fixed particle on the string and notes that it takes exactly $0.22\text{ s}$ to travel from its maximum crest position down to its lowest trough position. What is the frequency of the wave?
questions.explanation: Crest to trough is half a cycle. Full period $T = 2 \times 0.22 = 0.44\text{ s}$. $f = 1/T = 1/0.44 \approx 2.27\text{ Hz}$.

questions.question: A sound wave traveling through an open liquid column has an oscillation frequency of $1,350\text{ Hz}$ and a corresponding wavelength of $1.12\text{ m}$. How much time will it take for this sound wave to travel a linear distance of $3,780\text{ m}$ through the medium?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered advanced wave and optics problem-solving!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review mirror geometry and the crest-to-trough half-period concept.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on multi-step problems involving Snell's Law and critical angle.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: law of reflection, $n = c/v$, Snell's Law, and the wave equation $v = f\lambda$.
